records.ety: [L. levir a husband's brother, brother-in-law; akin to Gr. dah`r: cf. F. lvirat leviration.]

defs: Of, pertaining to, or in accordance with, a law of the ancient Israelites and other tribes and races, according to which a woman, whose husband died without issue, was married to the husband's brother.
quotes: The firstborn son of a leviratical marriage was reckoned and registered as the son of the deceased brother.
